title: WHO Global Health Observatory — infant mortality, maternal mortality, physicians, hospital beds, target country set
source_org: World Health Organization (WHO) Global Health Observatory (GHO)
download_url: https://ghoapi.azureedge.net/api/{INDICATOR_CODE}?$filter=SpatialDim eq 'IRN' or SpatialDim eq 'KOR' or ... (10 countries)

files.name: MDG_0000000001_Infant_mortality_rate.json
files.description: Infant mortality rate (probability of dying between birth and age 1, per 1000 live births)

files.name: MDG_0000000026_Maternal_mortality_ratio.json
files.description: Maternal mortality ratio (per 100,000 live births), WHO/UN modeled estimate

files.name: HWF_0001_Medical_doctors_per10000.json
files.description: Medical doctors (per 10,000 population) — Health Workforce family; divide by 10 for per-1000 figure

files.name: WHS6_102_Hospital_beds_per10000.json
files.description: Hospital beds (per 10,000 population); divide by 10 for per-1000 figure

time_coverage: 1949-2023 (varies by indicator: infant mortality 1949-2023, maternal mortality 1985-2023, medical doctors 1990-2023, hospital beds 2000-2023)
units: per 1000 live births (infant mortality); per 100,000 live births (maternal mortality); per 10,000 population (medical doctors, hospital beds — task asked for 'per 1000' but WHO's canonical GHO indicator codes for these two are natively per-10,000; simple /10 conversion gives per-1000)
notes: Retrieved via keyless WHO GHO OData API (ghoapi.azureedge.net), same approach as who-health-expenditure dataset, filtered server-side to our 10 target countries. Task-requested 'physicians per 1000 population' indicator code HRH_26 ('Physicians density (per 1000 population)') is registered in the GHO Indicator list but returned 0 rows both filtered AND unfiltered (globally empty in this API) — substituted HWF_0001 'Medical doctors (per 10,000)' from the newer Health Workforce (HWF) indicator family, which IS populated (261 rows, all 10 countries, 1990-2023); values are per 10,000, divide by 10 to get per-1000. Similarly used WHS6_102 'Hospital beds (per 10,000 population)' (also natively per-10,000, not per-1000 as literally requested in the task — no per-1000-specific hospital-bed indicator code exists in the GHO registry). Infant mortality (MDG_0000000001) has the deepest time series (back to 1949) and largest row count (2046) since it's a long-maintained MDG-era modeled estimate series. Maternal mortality ratio (MDG_0000000026) is the WHO/UN Maternal Mortality Estimation Inter-agency Group (MMEIG) modeled estimate; an alternate 'country reported estimates' variant (MDG_0000000032) exists but was not downloaded (task asked for one series; modeled estimate is the more internationally comparable one and is WHO's headline series).

failures.error: HTTP 200, 0 rows (globally empty, confirmed with unfiltered query)
